title: Liatris flabellata
taxonomy: Liatris flabellata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family. It belongs to the genus Liatris, which consists of several species of blazing star plants. Liatris flabellata is further classified as a member of the tribe Astereae. The plant is native to the southeastern United States and is often found in dry woodlands and rocky outcrops. Its classification as a species within the Liatris genus is based on its unique characteristics and physical features.
uses: Liatris flabellata, also known as the prairie blazing star, has been traditionally used in various medicinal applications. The plant's roots and leaves are reported to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat various skin conditions. In addition, Liatris flabellata is said to have antiseptic and antibacterial properties, making it effective in treating wounds and infections. The plant is also believed to have a calming effect on the digestive system, which can be beneficial in treating stomach issues and diarrhea. However, more research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Liatris flabellata for medicinal purposes.

constituents: Liatris flabellata, also known as the flat blazing star, is a plant that belongs to the sunflower family. The active constituents of Liatris flabellata include sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. intro: Liatris flabellata, also known as the drooping blazing star, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Liatris genus, commonly found in dry to moist prairies and open woods throughout the eastern United States. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, offering potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and alleviating respiratory issues, due to its expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Liatris flabellata include sesquiterpene lactones, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include dried roots and rhizomes, which are often used to make teas, decoctions, and infusions for medicinal purposes.
